Agent OS: Integrating NotebookLM and Obsidian Memory Systems

1 source·6 Jun 2026

The provided text outlines a sophisticated productivity ecosystem that integrates NotebookLM with Obsidian to create a unified artificial intelligence dashboard. By connecting these tools, users can transition from isolated AI interactions to a continuous memory system where agents retain business-specific context over time. NotebookLM serves as a powerful engine for content generation across multiple formats, while Obsidian acts as a local knowledge vault that prevents the AI from starting from zero during every session. This "Agent OS" workflow is designed to significantly reduce the manual labour involved in digital marketing and community management by automating the retrieval of past data. Ultimately, the source promotes a specialised framework that ensures AI outputs remain relevant, consistent, and deeply informed by the user's unique business goals.

How do NotebookLM and Obsidian create a shared memory system?
Explain the step-by-step workflow of the Agent OS dashboard.
What content formats can be generated using this AI setup?
Today • 21:05

Provide step by step process to integrate notebooklm with other tools

To integrate NotebookLM with other tools like Obsidian, Claude, and Hermes, you need to set up an "Agent OS" system that acts as a central dashboard and memory layer.
Here is the process for integrating and using these tools together:
The Technical Setup Requirements Setting up the integration from scratch involves connecting several moving parts. To build this system correctly, you will need:
  • The Agent OS dashboard: The central interface that runs locally on your machine.
  • An MCP bridge for NotebookLM: The connector that wires NotebookLM into the dashboard.
  • An Obsidian Vault: A free note-taking app that acts as the shared "memory layer" for your AI agents.
  • A Hermes connection: For integrating additional AI agent capabilities.
Note: The creator of this setup offers a pre-built, ready-to-install zip file containing the entire configured system (Agent OS, NotebookLM panel, and Obsidian memory vault) through their "AI Profit Boardroom" community.
The Step-by-Step Integrated Workflow Once the system is wired together, here is the five-step practical workflow for using NotebookLM alongside other tools in the Agent OS:
  • Step 1: Open the Agent OS dashboard. This gives you a single screen with tabs for all your agents and tools, including Claude, Hermes, the NotebookLM panel, your asset library, and your Obsidian vault.
  • Step 2: Access the NotebookLM panel. Select the specific notebook you want to work from. You can access the studio tab to view every piece of content (like past podcasts, videos, or infographics) that has ever been generated from that notebook.
  • Step 3: Generate new content. Decide what asset you need (e.g., a new audio overview) and hit generate. Because the system is integrated, NotebookLM does the work and automatically pulls the file directly into your asset library with just one button, eliminating the need to copy and paste or switch tabs.
  • Step 4: Refine with other AI agents. You can take the content NotebookLM just created and instantly pass it to other tools within the same dashboard. For example, you can load a podcast file into the Hermes video agent to add an avatar, customize the voice, and turn it into a polished video.
  • Step 5: Automatically save to the shared memory. Every task completed and note taken by your AI agents is automatically saved back to your Obsidian vault. Obsidian acts as a shared brain, meaning the next time you use Claude or Hermes, they will already have the full context of your business, past projects, and goals without you needing to re-prompt them.
By creating this loop, the knowledge goes into NotebookLM, content is generated and refined by other agents, and the memory of the entire process is stored in Obsidian so the system continuously gets smarter over time.
